blocks.content: He drew from memory — her hand on his jacket. The brush she'd forgotten to put down. The small smudge of blue on her left index finger from when she'd touched the wet canvas.

He drew the way her face had looked just before she crossed the distance. Not an expression he could have named ten minutes before it happened — the particular openness of someone who has decided to arrive somewhere and has.
He didn't try to make it perfect. He tried to make it true.
Somewhere across town, Cassandra was looking at her hand — the faint blue paint still there, shadow of a mark, proof of an afternoon.
In his sketchbook, pencil moving quiet across paper:
